reasoning_content:
We need to determine if 263 is a prime number. A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. So we check if 263 has any divisors other than 1 and itself.

First, note that 263 is odd, so not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 2+6+3=11, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Last digit is not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check divisibility by 7: 7*37=259, 7*38=266, so not divisible by 7. Check 11: 11*23=253, 11*24=264, so no. 13: 13*20=260, 13*21=273, so no. 17: 17*15=255, 17*16=272, so no. 19: 19*13=247, 19*14=266, so no. 23: 23*11=253, 23*12=276, so no. 29: 29*9=261, 29*10=290, so no. 31: 31*8=248, 31*9=279, so no. 37: 37*7=259, 37*8=296, so no. 41: 41*6=246, 41*7=287, so no. 43: 43*6=258, 43*7=301, so no. 47: 47*5=235, 47*6=282, so no. 53: 53*4=212, 53*5=265, so no. 59: 59*4=236, 59*5=295, so no. 61: 61*4=244, 61*5=305, so no. 67: 67*3=201, 67*4=268, so no. 71: 71*3=213, 71*4=284, so no. 73: 73*3=219, 73*4=292, so no. 79: 79*3=237, 79*4=316, so no. 83: 83*3=249, 83*4=332, so no. 89: 89*2=178, 89*3=267, so no. 97: 97*2=194, 97*3=291, so no. The square root of 263 is about 16.2, so we only need to check primes up to 16. Actually, we need to check up to sqrt(263) ≈ 16.2, so primes up to 16: 2,3,5,7,11,13. We already checked those. None divide 263. Therefore 263 is prime.

So answer: yes.
